title: State-write concurrency hardening: unique tmp names + parallel regression test
action: Advisor pre-Gate-3 finding 1 (codex-native-runtime-v2): bee-state-sync performs a full read-modify-write of .bee/state.json per matched PostToolUse event; writeJsonAtomic (templates/lib/fsutil.mjs ~:70, mirrored at .bee/bin/lib/fsutil.mjs) uses ONE FIXED .tmp filename, so concurrent sessions/hook invocations can collide (tmp clobber, last-writer-wins on interleaved rename). Before cnr2-2 widens the matcher to update_plan (which fires far more often in Codex sessions), harden the writer: make the tmp name unique per invocation (pid + monotonic/random suffix, same directory so rename stays atomic on the same filesystem), keeping the write-then-rename contract and fsync behavior identical. Edit the TEMPLATE source and sync the .bee/bin mirror the sanctioned way so test_lib_mirror stays green. Then write scripts/test_state_write_concurrency.mjs: spawn N>=8 concurrent writers hammering writeJsonAtomic on one target (and a state-sync-shaped read-modify-write loop), assert the file is never truncated/corrupt JSON at any point and no writer crashes (ENOENT on rename race is the classic failure with the fixed name). A single-process 'unrelated fields preserved' test is NOT sufficient (advisor explicit). NOTE the honest limit in the test header: unique tmp names remove the collision/corruption class; they do NOT serialize logical read-modify-write races (last-writer-wins remains, pre-existing on Claude today) — that broader serialization is out of scope here and stays a named limitation.

truths: writeJsonAtomic survives >=8 concurrent writers with zero corrupt/truncated reads and zero crashes

artifacts.substantive: true parallel writers, corruption + crash assertions, honest-limit header

key_links: fsutil tmp name unique per invocation, rename target unchanged
prohibitions: No locking/serialization redesign of state.mjs (named out of scope)
No behavior change to the written JSON content or fsync contract

verify: bash -c 'node scripts/test_state_write_concurrency.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && echo CONCURRENCY-OK'

trace.outcome: writeJsonAtomic (templates/lib/fsutil.mjs + .bee/bin/lib mirror) now uses a per-invocation unique tmp name (pid+counter+random) instead of one fixed <file>.tmp, closing the tmp-clobber/rename-race window flagged by the advisor pre-Gate-3 finding. Added scripts/test_state_write_concurrency.mjs: 18 real OS-process writers (10 hammer + 8 state-sync-shaped read-modify-write) hammer one shared target with a concurrent JSON-validity monitor; zero crashes, zero corrupt/truncated reads. Regenerated release-manifest.json (crit-pattern 20260715, lib file touched).

  "red_failure_evidence": "Before: writeJsonAtomic used ONE FIXED tmp name `${file}.tmp` (git-tracked prior content of skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/fsutil.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/fsutil.mjs, function at ~L70-75). Reverted .bee/bin/lib/fsutil.mjs to that exact prior content and reran the new test unchanged: 9/10 hammer workers crashed with 'Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, rename .../shared-state.json.tmp -> .../shared-state.json' at fs.renameSync in writeJsonAtomic (fsutil.mjs:74) -- the exact rename-race/tmp-clobber failure mode the advisor named. Restored the fixed content (unique per-invocation tmp name) and reran: PASS, 0 crashes, 0 corrupt/truncated/empty reads across all 3 repeated runs.",
  "verification_run": "bash -c 'node scripts/test_state_write_concurrency.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && echo CONCURRENCY-OK' -> PASS test_state_write_concurrency (18 processes, 0 crashes, 0 corrupt reads); PASS test_lib_mirror (byte-identical, 19 files); test_lib.mjs 343 passed/0 failed; CONCURRENCY-OK. Also: node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write then --check -> 146 files match; node scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs and node scripts/test_release_tuple.mjs both PASS (unaffected, run as collateral check since a lib file changed).",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["Logical read-modify-write races (last-writer-wins) are NOT serialized by this change -- explicitly out of scope per the cell (no locking/serialization redesign of state.mjs); documented in the shipped test's honest-limit header."]
}
